title: 中考六选五 18: The Meaning of Fairness
passage:
Students often talk about fairness. They may say it is unfair when someone gets more praise, a better seat or an easier task. 1. ______ But fairness is not always as simple as giving everyone exactly the same thing.

Sometimes fairness means treating people differently according to their needs. For example, a student with poor eyesight may need to sit closer to the blackboard. 2. ______ Giving the same seat to everyone may look equal, but it may not truly help everyone learn.

Fairness also includes responsibility. If two students work on a project, but only one of them makes real effort, equal praise may not be fair. 3. ______ In this case, fairness means noticing both the result and the effort behind it.

However, people may disagree about what is fair. Each person usually sees the situation from his or her own side. 4. ______ That is why calm communication is important when students feel something is unfair.

A mature understanding of fairness requires more than emotion. Students need to ask: What are the facts? What does each person need? Who has taken responsibility? 5. ______ These questions can help them judge fairness more wisely.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: The harder-working student may feel that his or her effort has been ignored.
This feeling is common, especially when students compare themselves with others.
Answering them can prevent students from making quick and one-sided judgments.
Different needs may require different support.
As a result, the same event may be understood in different ways.
Fairness means everyone should always receive exactly the same result.
